core_payload.content:
# DeepMine Project

## 1. Dream Direction
The Dream branch pushes DeepMine Project toward a fuller quarry fabric: richer provider diversity, stronger context-pack assembly, smarter ore-manifest generation, better assignment-aware routing, and more deliberate night-shift economics.

## 2. Desired Future State
- hybrid `api + subscription + hosted inference` lanes feeding one governed quarry.
- context packs assembled intentionally instead of dumping raw vault mass into the model.
- ore manifests that preserve evidence, extraction boundaries, and downstream refinement expectations.
- routing logic that understands user-facing, background, swarm, and repository-assignment work as distinct economic lanes.
- batch and night-shift execution that materially improves cost-to-signal performance.
- quarry output that is easier for A2C, Workspace, and N-Infinity to consume without folklore.

## 3. Why It Matters
Dream preserves the idea that DeepMine should become more than a connector bundle. It should mature into an industrial extraction fabric that supplies the rest of N1Hub with cleaner, deeper, more inspectable, and more economically rational raw material.

neuro_concentrate.summary: DeepMine Project in the Dream branch focuses on the future quarry fabric: broader provider diversity, stronger context-pack assembly, ore-manifest discipline, assignment-aware routing, and better cost-to-signal optimization across heavy intelligence workloads. The branch matters because current DeepMine infrastructure is only the first industrial layer of what a true cognitive quarry could become. In Dream, the project evolves toward a governed mining engine that supplies the rest of the ecosystem with deeper, cleaner, more inspectable, and more strategically filtered raw material.
